candidate_sense: prove or be discovered to be a certain way
near_miss_design_note: Same-domain contrast between finding/result and object/event that cannot turn out to be a property.
risk_note: Avoid transitive turn out meaning produce.

candidate_sense: discuss or deal with an issue idea or matter
near_miss_design_note: Use institutional or academic agenda contexts such as committee issue vs committee deadline.
risk_note: Can also mean occupy time or start activity, so context must block alternate senses.

candidate_sense: come to understand or determine something
near_miss_design_note: Use problem solving contexts such as cause explanation solution vs document surface feature.
risk_note: Very familiar but still C1-useful if near-miss is subtle.

candidate_sense: stop involvement temporarily to consider objectively
near_miss_design_note: Use decision analysis contexts such as manager stepped back from debate vs manager stepped back from answer.
risk_note: Physical step back sense must be blocked by abstract context.

candidate_sense: cease resistance or yield to demands
near_miss_design_note: Use negotiation contexts such as union gave in to demands vs union gave in to evidence.
risk_note: May be easier than ideal for C1, but stable and low polysemy.

candidate_sense: happen or develop over time
near_miss_design_note: Use process/event contexts such as negotiations played out publicly vs documents played out publicly.
risk_note: Needs event subject; avoid theatrical enactment reading.

candidate_sense: solve a problem or understand causes
near_miss_design_note: Use problem/conflict/reason contexts such as team sorted out dispute vs team sorted out prediction.
risk_note: Two related senses should be separated in item notes.

candidate_sense: examine or discuss each part in detail
near_miss_design_note: Use review/check contexts such as lawyer went over contract vs lawyer went over rumor.
risk_note: Can have literal movement sense, so use abstract object.

candidate_sense: make something public or available, or make a quality noticeable
near_miss_design_note: Use publication or feature-salience contexts such as publisher brought out edition vs publisher brought out deadline.
risk_note: Two source senses compete; select one target sense only.

candidate_sense: reject or refuse to consider, especially by authority
near_miss_design_note: Use legal or committee contexts such as judge threw out claim vs judge threw out witness.
risk_note: Also literal discard; institutional subject should block rubbish sense.

candidate_sense: continue doing or being involved despite difficulty
near_miss_design_note: Use continuation contexts such as team carried on working vs team carried on result.
risk_note: Common and stable; may be slightly easy for C1.

candidate_sense: make opportunities access or possibilities available
near_miss_design_note: Use opportunity/access contexts such as policy opened up options vs policy opened up objections.
risk_note: Also literal open; abstract object required.

candidate_sense: start a new topic/activity or recover from difficulty
near_miss_design_note: Use discourse transition contexts such as chair moved on to budget vs chair moved on to evidence.
risk_note: Select discourse-transition sense; avoid emotional recovery unless targeted.

candidate_sense: increase or strengthen progressively
near_miss_design_note: Use accumulation contexts such as tension built up before vote vs tension built up the memo.
risk_note: Increase sense can still be cueable; near-miss must avoid simple quantity reversal.

candidate_sense: stop a machine business system or process operating
near_miss_design_note: Use institutional or technical contexts such as agency shut down program vs agency shut down reason.
risk_note: Can be literal computer action; choose abstract organization/process object.

candidate_sense: refuse or reject offer request opportunity
near_miss_design_note: Use offer/opportunity/request contexts such as board turned down proposal vs board turned down evidence.
risk_note: Also volume-control sense exists but is easily blocked.

candidate_sense: gain control management or responsibility
near_miss_design_note: Use control/responsibility contexts such as deputy took over project vs deputy took over advice.
risk_note: Very stable; ensure object is controllable responsibility or organization.

candidate_sense: establish create or arrange something
near_miss_design_note: Use organization or arrangement contexts such as team set up committee vs team set up conclusion.
risk_note: Also physical placement; abstract institutional object should block.

candidate_sense: undertake role task responsibility problem or issue
near_miss_design_note: Use responsibility contexts such as analyst took on project vs analyst took on evidence.
risk_note: Also acquire property sense; object selection must block.

candidate_sense: gain control management or possession
near_miss_design_note: Use management transfer contexts such as firm took over contract vs firm took over statistic.
risk_note: Do not duplicate if selected as primary.

candidate_sense: raise for discussion or consideration
near_miss_design_note: Use meeting/discussion contexts such as senator brought up issue vs senator brought up outcome.
risk_note: Also child-rearing sense; meeting context blocks.

candidate_sense: end up in a particular situation, often unexpectedly
near_miss_design_note: Use outcome contexts such as company wound up paying fees vs company wound up the evidence.
risk_note: Can also mean close company or tighten toy; context must block.

candidate_sense: experience a difficult process or be approved
near_miss_design_note: Use difficult-experience sense with person/institution subject, or approval sense with bill/proposal subject.
risk_note: Must select one sense only; approval sense may be highly domain-specific.

candidate_sense: delay or prevent progress
near_miss_design_note: Use traffic/process contexts such as dispute held up talks vs dispute held up summary.
risk_note: Also physical holding and durability senses; context must be procedural.

candidate_sense: be successfully communicated or understood
near_miss_design_note: Use communication contexts such as message got through to audience vs message got through to deadline.
risk_note: Can mean phone contact or overcome difficulty; target sense must be explicit in notes.

candidate_sense: complete a form or official document
near_miss_design_note: Use official document contexts such as applicant filled out form vs applicant filled out policy.
risk_note: Stable but may be too easy for C1 unless used as low-anchor item.

candidate_sense: communicate information or transmit tradition
near_miss_design_note: Use information-transfer contexts such as manager passed on message vs manager passed on silence.
risk_note: Also euphemism die; avoid human subject without object.

candidate_sense: cause event reaction alarm or dispute to start
near_miss_design_note: Use causation contexts such as comment set off debate vs comment set off report.
risk_note: Also travel/departure and alarm senses; context must target causation.

candidate_sense: reduce level or cause loss of power/status
near_miss_design_note: Use rate/power contexts such as scandal brought down minister vs scandal brought down estimate.
risk_note: Physical downward sense must be blocked.

candidate_sense: delay until later
near_miss_design_note: Use scheduling contexts such as board put off vote vs board put off evidence.
risk_note: Also cause dislike; scheduling context blocks.

candidate_sense: happen or occur unexpectedly
near_miss_design_note: Use causal/explanatory contexts such as change came about gradually vs change came about report.
risk_note: May be broad and slightly easy; use only if final list needs event-process items.

candidate_sense: stop a business service or organization operating
near_miss_design_note: Use institutional contexts such as city closed down clinic vs city closed down reason.
risk_note: Stable but may overlap with shut down.

candidate_sense: provide missing information to someone
near_miss_design_note: Use information-update contexts such as assistant filled in director on plan vs assistant filled in director on chair.
risk_note: Also temporary replacement and material-fill senses; syntax must target information sense.

candidate_sense: convert to an opinion or decision
near_miss_design_note: Use opinion-change contexts such as committee came around to proposal vs committee came around to schedule.
risk_note: Also visit and recurring-event senses; context must block.

candidate_sense: start suddenly, especially undesirable event
near_miss_design_note: Use event onset contexts such as protests broke out downtown vs protests broke out policy.
risk_note: May be event-type cueable; use cautiously.

candidate_sense: leave meeting or event suddenly or angrily
near_miss_design_note: Use meeting/protest contexts such as delegates walked out of talks vs delegates walked out of conclusion.
risk_note: Physical leaving is part of sense; may be too transparent.

candidate_sense: circulate, especially rumor or information
near_miss_design_note: Use information-circulation contexts such as rumor went around campus vs rumor went around deadline.
risk_note: May rely on subject type; avoid if near-miss is too noun-category based.

candidate_sense: appear or seem in a particular way
near_miss_design_note: Use impression contexts such as speaker came off confident vs speaker came off conclusion.
risk_note: Also detach and finish-season senses; context must target impression.

candidate_sense: make someone leave an organization or reject something
near_miss_design_note: Use authority contexts; choose reject-object sense or expel-person sense, not both.
risk_note: Do not duplicate if selected as primary.

candidate_sense: increase or strengthen progressively
near_miss_design_note: Use accumulation contexts such as pressure built up during talks vs pressure built up agreement.
risk_note: Do not duplicate if selected as primary.
